source.title: Enhanced emotional interference on working memory performance in adults with ADHD

study.title: Enhanced emotional interference on working memory performance in adults with ADHD
study.population: Adults with ADHD and matched healthy controls.
study.design: Group comparison using an n-back working-memory task with emotionally salient distractors.
study.notes: PubMed reports lower n-back performance accuracy in ADHD patients relative to controls; indexed abstract records describe 1-back and 2-back conditions.

samples.label: Adults with ADHD
samples.population: Adults aged 18 to 40 years with ADHD.
samples.inclusionCriteria: Adult ADHD group reported in indexed abstract records for the emotional n-back study.
samples.exclusionCriteria: Not extracted from the abstract-level source.
samples.notes: Sample size comes from an indexed conference abstract record for the same emotional n-back study line; verify against the full article before quantitative reuse.

samples.label: Matched healthy controls
samples.inclusionCriteria: Matched healthy control group reported in indexed abstract records for the emotional n-back study.
samples.exclusionCriteria: Not extracted from the abstract-level source.
samples.notes: Sample size comes from an indexed conference abstract record for the same emotional n-back study line; verify against the full article before quantitative reuse.

tasks.label: Emotional n-back task
tasks.taskVariant: 1-back and 2-back working-memory task with emotionally salient distractors.
tasks.notes: Boundary's current n-back task is spatial and non-emotional, so this source is clinical context rather than a direct reference distribution.

measures.label: N-back performance accuracy
measures.transformation: Abstract-level extraction only; no numeric effect size or distribution has been extracted.
measures.notes: PubMed reports lower n-back performance accuracy for ADHD patients than controls, especially under emotionally salient distractors.

results.notes: Qualitative extraction only; numeric accuracy values and condition-specific effects are not represented.
Qualitative extraction only; numeric accuracy values and condition-specific effects are not represented.

comparisonClaims.claim: Adult ADHD studies can show lower n-back accuracy than matched controls under emotionally salient distractor conditions.
comparisonClaims.guardrail: Do not use this source to classify a Boundary participant as ADHD-like; the task variant is emotional, group-level, and not numerically extracted.

guardrails: This is abstract-level clinical-context extraction, not a direct Boundary reference distribution.
The task used emotionally salient distractors, unlike Boundary's current spatial n-back task.
Do not generate diagnosis, screening, or ADHD-resemblance prompts from this extraction until full-text numeric results and compatibility are reviewed.
reviewerNotes: Second extraction seed to exercise clinical group/sample/claim modeling. It deliberately remains internal-review only.
